name: appendMarkdown
description: Appends formatted content to the end of a document using markdown syntax. Supports headings, bold, italic, strikethrough, links, and bullet/numbered lists. Use this instead of appendText when you need formatting.

documentId.description: The document ID — the long string between /d/ and /edit in a Google Docs URL.

addNewlineIfNeeded.description: Add spacing before appended content if needed.

tabId.description: The ID of the specific tab to append to. If not specified, appends to the first tab.

firstHeadingAsTitle.description: If true, the first H1 heading (# ...) in the markdown is styled as a Google Docs TITLE instead of Heading 1. Useful when the markdown represents a full document whose first line is the document title.
